title: triniochloa alpestris
intro: Triniochloa alpestris, also known as Alpine Club-grass, is a type of grass native to the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Triniochloa alpestris include its ability to soothe respiratory tract infections, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. Triniochloa alpestris is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
